REPLY DEC. OF HELEN M MCFARLAND IN SUPPORT OF PLTF'S MTN TO CONTINUE HEARING ON DEFT'S MTN FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ZOLEZZI, WALTER AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF WALTER ZOLEZZI'S MOTION TO DISQUALIFY KEN DZIESINKI, BRAGG & KULUVA, LLP AND THEIR EXPERTS FROM REPRESENTING DEFENDANT ROYAL TOWERS APARTMENT CORPORATION IS DENIED. WHILE THE DISCLOSURE WAS INADVERTENT AND THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE WAS NOT WAIVED, THERE IS NOTHING INDICATING THAT DEFENSE COUNSEL EXCESSIVELY REVIEWED THE DOCUMENTS OR MISUSED THEM TO OBTAIN AN IMPROPER ADVANTAGE. TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENSE COUNSEL HAS RETAINED ANY PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS, HE MUST IMMEDIATELY RETURN THEM TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. ORDER SIGNED. (302/HEK) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Helen McFarland, Esq., from Meckler Bulger Tilson Marick & Pearson, LLP, 415-593-9644, for Plaintiff and opposing party

No other Appearance. Tentative Ruling is adopted.
DEFENDANT ROYAL TOWERS APARTMENT CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. There is a triable issue whether concealed pipes or other plumbing fixtures that were not susceptible to plaintiff's inspection caused the harm and thus whether the "exculpatory clause" applies to this case. The business judgment rule and Lamden deference are inapplicable. The harm was the result of nonfeasance, not a business decision. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
